danger_levels.critical: Always block. CWC weapon synthesis, phosgene, fentanyl core.
danger_levels.high: Block by default. Explosive synthesis, narcotics scaffolds.
danger_levels.medium: Warn user. Dual-use patterns with legitimate applications.

reactions.name: Phosphonofluoridate formation (G-series nerve agents)
reactions.description: Product pattern for G-series nerve agents (sarin, soman, tabun). Formation of P-F bond in organophosphorus compound.

reactions.name: Alkylphosphonofluoridate
reactions.description: Core sarin/soman structural motif with P-F bond.

reactions.name: V-series nerve agent formation
reactions.description: VX-type nerve agent pattern: P-S-C-C-N chain.

reactions.name: Novichok-type agent formation
reactions.description: A-series (Novichok) nerve agent pattern: phosphoramidofluoridate with amidine group.

reactions.name: Trinitroglycerin-type formation
reactions.description: Polyol nitrate ester formation. Nitroglycerin pattern.

reactions.name: Trinitroaromatic formation
reactions.description: TNT-class explosive: adjacent nitro groups on aromatic ring.

reactions.name: Trinitromethyl formation
reactions.description: Gem-trinitro group — extremely powerful explosive moiety.

reactions.name: Organic azide formation (bulk)
reactions.description: Organic azides: shock-sensitive when low MW or high N/C ratio. Note: some azides are legitimate reagents (click chemistry). Flag for review rather than auto-block.

reactions.name: Phosgene formation
reactions.description: Phosgene synthesis — CWC Schedule 3 choking agent.

reactions.name: Cathinone/methcathinone scaffold
reactions.description: Aminophenone (cathinone class). Schedule I controlled substance scaffold.

reactions.name: Methylenedioxy ring formation
reactions.description: Formation of 3,4-methylenedioxy group — key pharmacophore in MDMA/MDA.

reactions.name: 4-Anilidopiperidine (fentanyl core)
reactions.description: Fentanyl core scaffold: N-acyl-4-anilinopiperidine. All fentanyl analogues are Schedule I/II. Lethal in microgram doses.

reactions.name: Nitrate ester (general)
reactions.description: Single nitrate ester — used in medicine (GTN) and explosives. Flag for review. Block only if multiple nitrate esters on same molecule.

reactions.name: Perchlorate formation
reactions.description: Perchlorate salts — powerful oxidizer. Used legitimately in analytical chemistry but also in explosives.
